content.problemStatement:
You are an evil sorcerer at a round table with NNN sorcerers (including yourself). You can cast MMM spells which have distinct powers p1,p2,…,pMp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Mp1​,p2​,…,pM​.

You may perform the following operation any number of times (possibly zero):


Assign a living sorcerer to each positive integer cyclically to your left starting from yourself ― the closest living sorcerer to your left is assigned to 111, the next living sorcerer to the left is assigned to 222 and so on. Note that each living sorcerer (including yourself) is assigned to an infinite number of integers.
Choose a spell jjj (possibly a spell you have chosen before) and kill the living sorcerer assigned to pjp_jpj​. You may not cast a spell to kill yourself.
What is the maximum number of sorcerers you can kill using zero or more operations?
constraints: 1 ≤ M ≤ 3·10^5 (sum of M over all test cases ≤ 3·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and M — the number of sorcerers (including yourself) and the number of spells. The second line contains M distinct integers p1,p2,…,pM — the powers of the spells.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of sorcerers you can kill.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case compute g = gcd of spells, find the largest divisor of g not exceeding N, and answer = N - that divisor. The results match the sample output.

solution.approach: For a current number of living sorcerers k, a spell with power p kills the sorcerer assigned to p mod k. You cannot kill yourself, i.e. you cannot use a spell whose power is a multiple of k. Therefore you can decrease k as long as there exists a spell with p mod k ≠ 0. The process stops exactly when k divides every spell power. All common divisors of the spell powers are precisely the divisors of g = gcd(p1,…,pM). Hence the smallest reachable number of living sorcerers is the largest divisor d of g that does not exceed the initial N. The answer is N‑d. Algorithm: compute g = gcd of all pi. Enumerate all divisors of g in O(√g) and keep the maximum divisor ≤ N. Return N‑maxDiv.

code.Python3:
import sys, math
def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data: return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); M = int(next(it))
        g = 0
        for i in range(M):
            p = int(next(it))
            g = p if i==0 else math.gcd(g, p)
        best = 1
        d = 1
        while d*d <= g:
            if g % d == 0:
                d1 = d
                d2 = g // d
                if d1 <= N and d1 > best:
                    best = d1
                if d2 <= N and d2 > best:
                    best = d2
            d += 1
        out.append(str(N - best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
